config.roadweaver.a_star_max_steps.toolt: Maximum search steps for A* algorithm per generation to prevent infinite loops in complex terrain.
config.roadweaver.a_star_step.tooltip: Grid step between sampled neighbors for A*. Larger values reduce nodes and improve performance but may reduce path fidelity. Default 16.
config.roadweaver.allow_artificial.toolt: If enabled, the mod will pave road surfaces. Disable to prevent paving (useful for planning or markers only).
config.roadweaver.allow_natural.tooltip: If enabled, the mod will pave natural road surfaces. Disable to prevent paving (useful for planning or markers only).
config.roadweaver.averaging_radius.toolt: Average height samples across N neighboring segments to reduce noise before smoothing.
config.roadweaver.biome_prefilter.toolti: Prefilter by allowed biomes of selected structures to reduce false positives and checks.
config.roadweaver.biome_weight.tooltip: Weight for biome cost (e.g., rivers, oceans).
config.roadweaver.bridge_enabled.tooltip: Build elevated decks over water spans identified by the path analyzer.
config.roadweaver.bridge_keep_lamps.tool: If enabled, keep road lamps/markers on bridge spans.
config.roadweaver.bridge_max_length_bloc: If a water span is longer than this value, the entire span will be skipped (no bridge and no road). Set to 0 for unlimited.
config.roadweaver.bridge_merge_gap.toolt: Merge nearby bridge spans whose gap is at most this many segments to avoid frequent up-and-down decks over small islands or sand bars.
config.roadweaver.bridge_min_length.tool: Minimum span length (in road segments) required to actually place a bridge. Shorter water runs are treated as normal terrain.
config.roadweaver.bridge_min_water_depth: Only build bridges over water columns whose depth (sea level minus ocean floor) is at least this many blocks. Helps ignore shallow puddles.
config.roadweaver.bridge_pier_interval.t: Place supporting piers every N segments along the bridge.
config.roadweaver.bridge_pier_max_height: Stop extending a pier downward once this height is reached.
config.roadweaver.bridge_pier_width.tool: Square cross-section width for each pier (1-3).
config.roadweaver.bridge_ramp_segments.t: Number of segments used to smoothly ramp from ground height to deck height at both bridge ends.
config.roadweaver.bridge_use_buoys_inste: If enabled, place buoys on water spans instead of building a bridge.
config.roadweaver.bridge_use_buoys_when_: Use Buoys When Bridge Is Skipped
If enabled, when a bridge span is skipped due to exceeding max length, buoys will be placed on that span instead.
config.roadweaver.buoy_interval_blocks.t: Distance between buoys along a water span.
config.roadweaver.deviation_weight.toolt: Weight for deviation from a straight line. Higher values prefer straighter paths.
config.roadweaver.diag_step_cost.tooltip: Base cost for diagonal steps. Higher values discourage diagonal movement.
config.roadweaver.dynamic_plan_enabled.t: Periodically plan around players as new structures enter the configured radius.
config.roadweaver.dynamic_plan_radius_ch: Radius around each player (in chunks) to include structures in planning.
config.roadweaver.dynamic_plan_stride_ch: Step size (in chunks) for triggering dynamic planning as players move; larger stride triggers less frequently.
config.roadweaver.elevation_weight.toolt: Weight for elevation changes. Higher values prefer flatter terrain.
config.roadweaver.enable_prediction.tool: Predict structure locations based on world seed, filtered by whitelist/blacklist.
config.roadweaver.heuristic_weight.toolt: Weight for the heuristic. Higher values push the path more directly towards the goal.
config.roadweaver.hierarchical_pathfindi: When enabled, the pathfinder will first run a coarse A* with a fixed large step (64) to prewarm terrain sampling cache (noise height/biome/water). The coarse path is NOT used for road generation; the final road still uses the selected algorithm once.
config.roadweaver.highway_a_star_max_ste: Maximum search steps for highway pathfinding.
config.roadweaver.highway_a_star_step.to: Grid sampling step for highway pathfinding. Default 32.
config.roadweaver.highway_auto_plan_enab: When enabled, initial/dynamic planning will generate a grid of highway nodes based on the configured grid spacing and connect adjacent nodes (written to highwayConnections).
config.roadweaver.highway_dynamic_plan_e: When enabled, maintain a rolling 3x3 Highway cell window centered on the player and prune connections outside the window. When disabled, keep only the player's current 1x1 cell (follows the player).
config.roadweaver.highway_enabled.toolti: Enables an additional road type: Highway. Experimental feature, if terrain is not flat enough, please be careful to enable it. Generation is scheduled together with normal roads.
config.roadweaver.highway_floating_weigh: If the ideal height profile is above ground, treat it as viaduct/bridge and add cost = heightDelta * weight. Higher values discourage viaducts.
config.roadweaver.highway_grid_blocks.to: Place a highway grid node every N blocks. Smaller values create a denser grid and increase planning/generation cost.
config.roadweaver.highway_penetration_we: If the ideal height profile is below ground, treat it as cutting/tunnel and add cost = heightDelta * weight. Higher values discourage tunneling.
config.roadweaver.highway_slope_limit_en: Limit highway height changes using a configurable rise/run to smooth elevation.
config.roadweaver.highway_slope_rise_blo: Rise in blocks for highway slope limiting (default 1).
config.roadweaver.highway_slope_run_bloc: Run distance in blocks for highway slope limiting (default 5).
config.roadweaver.initial_plan_radius_ch: At new world start, build network around spawn within this radius (in chunks).
config.roadweaver.interpolated_roadbed_f: When enabled, terrain adaptation uses interpolated heights. Note: This setting only applies to normal roads; highways always skip fill.
config.roadweaver.lamp_interval.tooltip: Place a lamp/marker every N segments; larger values place fewer lamps.
config.roadweaver.large_structure_offset: Distance from road center for large structures in blocks.
config.roadweaver.loading_tips_enabled.t: Show RoadWeaver related tips and tricks in the bottom right corner of the world loading screen.
config.roadweaver.max_concurrent_generat: Cap the number of roads generating in parallel.
config.roadweaver.max_slope_step_per_two: Maximum Y change allowed across 2 segments when slope limiting is enabled.
config.roadweaver.max_structures_per_roa: Maximum number of roadside structures to place per road. Set to 0 to disable structures.
config.roadweaver.medium_structure_offse: Distance from road center for medium structures (e.g., coffee houses) in blocks.
Minimum distance between roadside structures in blocks. Larger values result in sparser distribution.
config.roadweaver.near_water_cost.toolti: Cost for being near water. Higher values prefer to stay away from water.
config.roadweaver.ortho_step_cost.toolti: Base cost for orthogonal steps. Higher values discourage orthogonal movement.
config.roadweaver.other_structure_road_o: Distance (in blocks) from road endpoints to non-village structure centers.
Choose which pathfinding algorithm to use when generating roads.
config.roadweaver.place_waypoints.toolti: Only place waypoints/markers instead of paving the road surface.
config.roadweaver.planning_algorithm.too: Choose the primary edge algorithm for road network planning: KNN = sparse local graph; Delaunay = more uniform but denser mesh; RNG = sparser skeleton; MST = tree with minimum total edge length.
config.roadweaver.planning_algorithm_del: When enabled, plan primary edges with Delaunay triangulation; otherwise use K-Nearest Neighbors.
config.roadweaver.planning_algorithm_knn: Plan primary edges with KNN (sparse, local connections).
config.roadweaver.planning_algorithm_mst: Plan primary edges with MST (tree with minimum total edge length).
config.roadweaver.planning_algorithm_rng: Plan primary edges with RNG (sparser skeleton).
When enabled, trees and large plants (bamboo, huge fungi) will not generate on or near roads. This prevents floating trees caused by road cutting.
config.roadweaver.radius_chunks.tooltip: Prediction radius centered at spawn, in chunks.
config.roadweaver.road_clear_height.tool: Vertical space cleared above the road surface in non-tunnel mode. Higher values carve more headroom but may cut into nearby terrain or structures.
config.roadweaver.road_fill_enabled.tool: When disabled, RoadWeaver will only place surface blocks. Note: Highway system no longer uses roadbed fill, this setting only applies to normal roads.
config.roadweaver.road_signs_enabled.too: When enabled, place distance signs near both ends of a road and place warning signs when an over-long sea crossing is skipped.
config.roadweaver.roads_enabled.tooltip: Master switch for the road system. When disabled, road planning/generation and feature placement will be skipped.
config.roadweaver.roadside_structures_en: When enabled, decorative structures (benches, campfires, coffee houses) will be placed along roads based on biome and road length.
config.roadweaver.slope_limit_enabled.to: When enabled, the road height profile will be smoothed by limiting steep slopes.
config.roadweaver.small_structure_offset: Distance from road center for small structures (e.g., benches, campfires) in blocks.
config.roadweaver.spawn_cabin_enabled: Enable connection to spawn point road
config.roadweaver.spawn_cabin_enabled.to: Connect the road from the spawn point to the surrounding structures.
config.roadweaver.stability_weight.toolt: Weight for terrain stability. Higher values avoid cliffs and pits.
config.roadweaver.structure_avoidance_en: When enabled, roads will automatically skip segments that fall within structure bounding boxes (e.g., village buildings), preventing roads from damaging structures.
config.roadweaver.structure_prediction_d: Select which dimensions allow structure prediction.
Distance (in blocks) from road endpoints to structure centers. Roads will start/end this far from village centers.
config.roadweaver.structure_selection.no: No structure data found. Please enter a world first to discover structures.
config.roadweaver.thread_duty_cycle.tool: CPU usage ratio for worker threads (1-100%). 100% = full speed; 50% = work 20ms then rest 20ms, etc. Lower values reduce CPU load but slow down road generation.
config.roadweaver.title: RoadWeaver Configuration
config.roadweaver.use_bidirectional_a_st: Toggle between classic single-direction A* and bidirectional A* that searches from both endpoints.
config.roadweaver.village_road_offset.to: Distance (in blocks) from road endpoints to village centers. Roads will start/end this far from villages.
config.roadweaver.water_depth_weight.too: Weight for water depth. Higher values avoid deep water.
config.roadweaver.water_proximity_cost.t: Graduated cost for proximity to water (0-3 layers). Higher values push paths further from shorelines. Each layer adds this cost.
gui.roadweaver.initgen.title: RoadWeaver Initial Generation
gui.roadweaver.map.note.title: Map Note
gui.roadweaver.map.title: Road Map
gui.roadweaver.preset_editor.name: Preset name
gui.roadweaver.preset_editor.title: Custom Road Material Preset
gui.roadweaver.roadside_browser.preview_: Preview unavailable (singleplayer only or template missing).
gui.roadweaver.roadside_browser.title: Roadside Structures
message.roadweaver.migration.done: [RoadWeaver] Legacy road data migration completed: %s roads.
message.roadweaver.migration.start: [RoadWeaver] Detected legacy road data. Migrating to SQLite...
text.autoconfig.roadweaver.option.comput: Threads used for background computation (planning, verification, map snapshots). 0 = auto (CPU-1), >0 = fixed thread count (1-128). Changes take effect after restarting the world/server.
text.autoconfig.roadweaver.option.genera: Number of worker threads for asynchronous road generation (1-64). Changes take effect after restarting the world/server.
text.autoconfig.roadweaver.option.initia: Dedicated thread count for initial road generation during world startup (1-64). Default 6.
tip.roadweaver.loading.1: Press H to open the RoadWeaver map.
tip.roadweaver.loading.2: You can configure the initial planning radius in the RoadWeaver settings.
tip.roadweaver.loading.3: A large initial planning radius may significantly slow down new world creation.
tip.roadweaver.loading.4: Initial loading is to ensure you have a complete road when entering the world.
tip.roadweaver.loading.5: Don't tp into the area where the road has not been paved!
